title: deschampsia hispanica subsp. gredensis
intro: Deschampsia hispanica subsp. gredensis, also known as Spanish hair grass or Gredos hair grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass native to the Iberian Peninsula, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Deschampsia hispanica subsp. gredensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations.
